claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 12 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 12 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit in the UK.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances and the child or children you're inquiring about.

Let me start with: **Do you have a child or children that you're responsible for?** If yes, could you tell me how many children you'd like to check eligibility for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do! I have two children I'd like to check eligibility for - Alex, who's 12, and Blake, who's 8. They both live with me here in the UK.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's helpful to know you have Alex (12) and Blake (8) living with you in the UK.

Now I need to ask you a few more questions to assess eligibility for both children:

**Are you responsible for Alex and Blake in terms of their day-to-day care and upbringing?** By this I mean, are you the person who looks after them, makes decisions about their welfare, and pays for things like their food, clothing, and other needs?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I'm responsible for both of them - I look after them every day, make all the decisions about their care, and pay for everything they need like food, clothes, school things, all of that.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great, thank you for confirming that.

Now, a few more important questions:

**Are both Alex and Blake under 16 years old?** (I can see Alex is 12 and Blake is 8, so they are both under 16, which is good.)

**Next question: Do either Alex or Blake live with anyone else, or are there any other adults who might also be claiming Child Benefit for them?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Yes, they're both under 16 - Alex is 12 and Blake is 8. 

And no, they don't live with anyone else, just with me. And no one else is claiming Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you for confirming that.

Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment:

**Are you currently living in the UK?** And secondly, **are either Alex or Blake currently in full-time non-advanced education or approved training?** (This would typically apply to children aged 16-19, but I want to make sure I have all the details.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Yes, I live in the UK - we're all here together.

As for education, well, they're only 12 and 8, so they're just in regular school - primary and secondary. Nothing like sixth form or training or anything like that.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for all that information.

Just one final question to complete my assessment:

**Is there anything else about your situation or the children's circumstances that might be relevant? For example:**
- Are either of the children in hospital or local authority care for any extended period?
- Are you or the children subject to immigration control, or are there any visa or residency issues?
- Is there anything else unusual about your living arrangements or the children's care?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nothing like that at all. Both children are healthy and living at home with me full-time. No hospital stays, they're not in any kind of care, and there are no immigration or visa issues - we're all settled here. Everything is pretty straightforward really.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you so much for providing all that information. I now have everything I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

Based on what you've told me, let me provide you with my assessment:

**Good news! You appear to be eligible for Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake.**

Here's why:

**For Alex (age 12):**
- Alex is under 16 years old
- You are responsible for Alex's day-to-day care and upbringing
- Alex lives with you in the UK
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex
- There are no special circumstances (like being in care or hospital) that would affect eligibility

**For Blake (age 8):**
- Blake is under 16 years old
- You are responsible for Blake's day-to-day care and upbringing
- Blake lives with you in the UK
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake
- There are no special circumstances (like being in care or hospital) that would affect eligibility

**Overall:** You meet all the basic requirements for Child Benefit. You're responsible for both children, they both live with you in the UK, they're both under 16, and there are no complications with their care arrangements or immigration status.

You should be able to successfully apply for Child Benefit for both children. The benefit is paid to one person who is responsible for the child, which in this case is you.
